title: The Skin Garden
summary: How the skin microbiome works — a calm, layered community of microbes that lives across every patch of skin. Trauma-informed register: no shame around eczema, acne, or body image; the skin is a garden, not a battlefield.

questions.prompt: The skin microbiome is the community of microbes that live…
choices: Across the whole surface of your skin — face, arms, scalp, everywhere
questions.explanation: Every patch of skin hosts its own neighborhood of microbes. Dry skin, oily skin, and damp skin each carry their own crowd — all part of you.

questions.prompt: Cutibacterium acnes is a skin microbe that lives near hair follicles. It usually…
choices: Keeps the skin balanced by living quietly on natural skin oils
questions.explanation: Cutibacterium acnes is on almost everyone — it feeds on the skin's natural oils and is mostly a quiet helper. Skin changes happen for many reasons; this microbe is one small part of a much bigger ecology.

questions.prompt: Demodex mites live in tiny numbers on almost every adult and most kids. They usually…
choices: Live quietly in skin pores and don't cause trouble
Live only in soil, never on people
questions.explanation: Demodex mites are part of the normal skin community for nearly everyone. They're tiny, they're quiet, and they're just one more member of the garden.

questions.prompt: Gentle washing helps the skin microbiome stay balanced because it…
choices: Removes excess oil and old skin cells without stripping the protective community
Makes the skin grow new layers instantly
questions.explanation: Skin doesn't need to be sterile — it needs to be cared for. Gentle washing keeps the community in balance; harsh scrubbing can disrupt it.

questions.prompt: Eczema, dry patches, and skin sensitivity are usually…
choices: A sign the skin community needs a little support — moisturizer, calm care, sometimes a doctor's help
A sign someone did something wrong
A reason to feel bad about yourself
questions.explanation: Skin sensitivities happen to many people, at every age. They don't mean anyone failed — the skin community just needs more care, and sometimes a grown-up or doctor to help figure out what.

questions.prompt: Most microbes in a healthy skin community are…
choices: Quiet members that help keep the skin's protective layer working
questions.explanation: The skin garden — like the gut and the mouth — is mostly quiet helpers. Everyone's skin community is slightly different, and that's part of what makes you, you.
